q: Henry Becquerel discovered radioactivity.
The term radioactivity is also known as nuclear disintegration.
Nuclear radiation is emitted from the nuclei of stable nuclides.
Uranium is a radioactive element.
Radioactive elements cannot be artificially produced.
The atomic number is conserved in the nuclear decay process.
The mass number is conserved in the nuclear decay process.
An electron is emitted in the beta minus decay process.
Radioactive decay is a random process.
Alpha rays are streams of helium nuclei.
Beta rays are streams of fast-moving electrons.
Gamma rays are electromagnetic waves of very short wavelength.
Alpha particles have a positive charge of +2e.
Beta minus particles have a negative charge of -1e.
Gamma rays are deflected by electric fields.
Alpha particles have the greatest ionizing power.
Gamma rays have the greatest penetrating power.
Alpha radiation can be stopped by a thin sheet of paper.
Beta radiation is stopped by a few millimetres of aluminium.
Gamma radiation is never completely absorbed by matter.
The activity of a radioactive source increases with time.
The SI unit of activity is the Becquerel (Bq).
The half-life of a radioactive isotope is constant.
Isotopes are atoms with the same number of protons but different number of neutrons.
A G-M counter is used to detect nuclear radiation.
Alpha particles produce thick, straight tracks in a cloud chamber.
Beta particles produce thin, curly tracks in a cloud chamber.
Cosmic rays are a source of background radiation.
Radioactive tracers are used in industry to detect leaks in pipes.
Cobalt-60 is used in radiotherapy to treat cancer.
Radioactive materials can be handled with bare hands safely.
Radioactive waste must be stored in lead-lined containers.
Nuclear fission is the splitting of a heavy nucleus into two lighter nuclei.
Nuclear fusion is the combining of two light nuclei to form a heavier nucleus.
A chain reaction occurs in nuclear fission.
Control rods in a nuclear reactor are used to speed up the reaction.
The energy of the sun comes from nuclear fusion.
Nuclear fusion requires very high temperatures.
Carbon-14 is used for dating ancient organic materials.
The mass of an alpha particle is approximately four times the mass of a proton.
Gamma radiation is emitted from an excited nucleus.
Beta particles are more penetrating than alpha particles.
Nuclear radiation can cause genetic mutations.
Radioisotopes used in medical imaging should have a very long half-life.
Radon gas is a natural source of background radiation.
One Becquerel is one disintegration per second.
In alpha decay, the mass number decreases by four.
In beta decay, the atomic number increases by one.
The speed of gamma rays is equal to the speed of light.
A moderator in a nuclear reactor slows down neutrons.
